title: Pick FIR or IIR by phase and stability needs
description: Choose FIR for guaranteed stability and linear phase, IIR when low order and sharp cutoff matter more than phase linearity.
body: Use when a filter spec lists both phase and order constraints. Do not reach for an IIR design when the signal carries timing-sensitive edges that phase distortion would smear. Actions: compare the required stopband attenuation against the order budget, run a linear-phase check on the FIR candidate, and record group-delay flatness. Validation signals include a measured constant group delay for the FIR path and a pole-radius test confirming every IIR pole sits inside the unit circle. Risk boundary: an unstable IIR ships oscillation into production, so deny any IIR design whose poles cannot be proven bounded.
